text.title: error-recovery Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for error-recovery as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Showing only a technical error code: "Error: ECONNREFUSED" or "Error 403".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Technical codes are meaningless to users. They cannot diagnose the problem or take any corrective action. Even "Connection refused" is better than the raw code.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tells users nothing about what failed, why, or what to do. Generates immediate support escalation because users have no information to self-serve.

failure-mode.description: Blame framing: "You entered invalid data" or "You did not complete the form correctly."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Accusatory language increases frustration without adding information. Replace with passive or system-focused framing: "This field requires a valid date" rather than "You entered an invalid date."

failure-mode.description: Dead-end error with only an [OK] button and no recovery path.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Users who dismiss an [OK] dialog are returned to an unchanged state with no guidance. They will either repeat the same action (same failure) or abandon. Always offer at least one forward path.
